phrases.intent_en: Ask a local to recommend a wine bar in the neighbourhood
phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous me recommander un bar à vins dans ce quartier ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-RYAY-voo muh ruh-koh-mahn-DAY un BAR ah VAN dahn suh kar-TYAY
phrases.cultural_note: Saint-Pierre is the historic heart of Bordeaux, packed with wine bars, restaurants, and medieval streets within the UNESCO zone.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for directions to the Place du Parlement
phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous m'indiquer le chemin vers la Place du Parlement ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-RYAY-voo mahn-dee-KAY luh shuh-MAN vair lah PLASS doo par-luh-MAHN
phrases.cultural_note: Place du Parlement is a beautiful 18th-century square in the Saint-Pierre quarter, lined with restaurant terraces.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if this restaurant has a terrace outside
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que votre restaurant dispose d'une terrasse en extérieur ?
phrases.pronunciation: es-kuh VOH-truh res-toh-RAHN dees-POHZ dyoon teh-RASS ahn ex-tay-RYUR
phrases.cultural_note: Outdoor dining on terraces is a Bordeaux tradition; the warm climate makes al fresco meals possible much of the year.

responses.english: Yes, we have a terrace in the sun.
responses.local: Oui, nous avons une terrasse au soleil.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the house wine is a local Bordeaux
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que le vin de la maison est un Bordeaux local ?
phrases.pronunciation: es-kuh luh VAN duh lah may-ZOHN eh un bor-DOH loh-KAL
phrases.cultural_note: In Bordeaux's Saint-Pierre quarter, nearly every restaurant prides itself on offering wines from the surrounding region.

responses.english: Yes, it is a red Bordeaux from the region.
responses.phonetic: bawn-ZHOOR luh VAN duh lah may-ZON ES-/ay eel loh-KAL
responses.local: Oui, c'est un bordeaux rouge de la région.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there is a table available for two without a reservation
phrases.text_local: Avez-vous une table disponible pour deux personnes sans réservation ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-VAY-voo yoon TAB-luh dees-poh-NEEBL poor duh pair-SON sahn ray-zair-VAH-SYON
phrases.cultural_note: Saint-Pierre is very popular at lunch and dinner; walking in without a reservation is possible but you may have to wait, especially on weekends.

responses.english: Yes, follow me, I will seat you.
responses.phonetic: bawn-ZHOOR AH-vay voo een TAH-bl poor DUH
responses.local: Oui, suivez-moi, je vais vous installer.
